figure.title: Dual Isolation Mode
figure.purpose: Shows both ACC A and ACC B originator components entering isolation mode after a link failure.

technical_summary: Following a link failure and subsequent timeouts/responses, the originators in both ACC A and ACC B are placed into Isolation Mode.

The UPU Originator blocks in both ACC A and ACC B are highlighted, showing circular arrows indicating they are handling traffic internally (dropping or looping back) rather than forwarding it to the failed link.

components.name: UPU Originator (ACC A)
components.description: Enters isolation mode on the left side.

components.name: UPU Originator (ACC B)
components.description: Enters isolation mode on the right side.

critical_facts: A single link failure can lead to multiple nodes entering isolation mode as they detect timeouts and receive isolation responses.
